name: Chicken Saltimbocca
recipeIngredient: 2 Boneless chicken breasts (each split in half)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the ground black pepper and buttermilk in a bowl or glass pie dish large enough to hold the chicken in one layer. Set aside.
2. Place flour on a plate for dusting.
3. Arrange a slice of ham under the skin of each chicken breast, and place 2 sage leaves (or some dried sage) inside each breast.
4. Dip the breasts into the buttermilk mixture and refrigerate for 20 minutes. Turn the breasts over and let them marinate another 20 minutes.
5. Heat cooking oil in a heavy skillet to a depth of about 1 inch, until it reaches 375°F. The oil is ready when a droplet of water bubbles and dances across the surface.
6. Remove the chicken from the refrigerator and take them out of the marinade, wiping off excess buttermilk.
7. Dust both sides of each breast with flour.
8. Place the breasts in the hot oil and cook for about 6 minutes, turning once to achieve a golden brown crust. Continue cooking for another 6-8 minutes until fully cooked.
9. Remove the breasts from the oil and drain on an absorbent towel.
10. Serve immediately.
